name: Vercel API
what_it_does: Vercel's REST API for programmatically managing deployments, projects, domains, environment variables, and team settings on the Vercel platform.
use_cases: Automating deployments from CI/CD pipelines via agents
Managing environment variables across projects and environments
Monitoring deployment status and build logs from agents
Managing custom domains and SSL certificates via automation
not_for: Long-running compute jobs (Vercel has execution time limits)
best_when: An agent needs to manage Vercel deployments, environment variables, or project configuration as part of a DevOps or CI/CD workflow.
avoid_when: You need persistent server processes, GPU compute, or are deploying non-JS/non-frontend workloads.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens for single-account use. OAuth2 for integrations needing multi-user access. Tokens are team-scoped. Very straightforward for agent use with PATs.

pricing.notes: Free Hobby tier has generous limits for personal projects. Pro required for team features. Commercial use requires Pro or Enterprise.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean JSON error responses with error code and message. Deployment build errors are retrievable from logs endpoint. Generally very agent-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deployments are inherently idempotent by git SHA. Environment variable operations are idempotent by key.
known_agent_gotchas: Hobby plan forbids commercial use — agents automating business workflows need Pro
Build logs require polling the deployment status endpoint — no push notification
Environment variables are encrypted at rest and cannot be read back after setting
Team ID vs personal account scoping requires careful parameter handling
Deployment URLs are immutable — each deploy gets a unique URL, aliases require separate call
